Supervised Learning:
- Involves modelling the relationship b/t FEATURES & LABEL of data

Unsupervised Learning:
- Involves modelling the FEATURES without any LABEL

Regression Task:

Clustering Task:

div.div:
Dimensionality Reduction Task:
- Seeks to pull out some low-dimensional representation of data
that in some way preserves relevant qualities of the full dataset

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 710px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: nowrap;

div.div:
FEATURES MATRIX:
- Represented as capital x (X)
- Shape: (n_samples, n_features)
- The samples (i.e., rows) refer to the individual objects described by the dataset
- The features (i.e., columns) refer to the distinct observations
that describe
each
sample in a quantitative manner.
- Features are generally real-valued, but may be
Boolean or categorical-valued

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 825px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: nowrap;

div.div:
TARGET ARRAY:
- Represented as small-case y (y)
- Shape: (n_samples, )
- Target array is usually the quantity we want to PREDICT
- Target array has continuous numerical values or discrete classes/labels
